pregame_model_read.pre_bet_thesis: Baz was priced as the best visible board value at +139 for 6+ with a model median around the threshold. The play depended on whiff-path activation and normal strikeout conversion against San Diego.

what_worked: Stake was controlled at standard size rather than laddered.
No 7+ ladder was correct because Baz finished with only 1 strikeout.
Cutter generated whiffs, showing some bat-missing existed in the profile.
what_failed: Strikeout conversion collapsed despite 103 pitches and 24 batters faced.
Overall CSW was only 24%, below the green zone required for a strong K path.
Baz produced 13 whiffs but only one strikeout, indicating poor putaway sequencing and/or Padres contact survival in two-strike counts.
Knuckle curve and sinker consumed 53 pitches but produced zero strikeouts.
risk_notes: This was a severe conversion miss, not a leash miss.
The profile should be tagged as whiffs-present-no-putaway because raw whiffs were not absent, but they failed to compound into strikeouts.
Future Baz overs should require early live two-strike conversion confirmation rather than relying solely on whiff projection.
model_evaluation.patch_recommendation: Add WHIFFS_PRESENT_NO_PUTAWAY penalty: when a pitcher has volatile putaway history or opponent contact survival risk, require live confirmation of strikeout conversion before standard staking 6+ markets. Penalize large pitch-count/workload profiles that produce whiffs without terminal Ks.
